articles.title: USS Gerald R. Ford Returns to Red Sea Operations
articles.description: Nuclear-powered carrier USS Gerald R. Ford resumed Red Sea operations after maintenance, now supporting Operation Epic Fury alongside USS Abraham Lincoln and USS Tripoli Strike Group.

articles.title: CENTCOM Hormus Blockade Operations and Mine Clearance Staging
articles.description: U.S. Navy repositioning 10,000+ personnel, destroyers, and minesweepers to clear Strait of Hormuz; USS George H.W. Bush diverted around Cape of Good Hope due to Houthi threats in Bab el-Mandeb.

articles.title: Experts Warn on Bab el-Mandeb Vulnerability Despite Hormuz Focus
articles.description: CSIS and Pentagon experts warn Houthis could close Bab el-Mandeb Strait amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire; CENTCOM refined blockade scope to Iranian-bound vessels only.

articles.title: USS George H.W. Bush Forced to Detour Around Africa
articles.description: Supercarrier diverted 6,000 miles around Cape of Good Hope due to sustained Houthi missile, drone, and small-boat threats in Bab el-Mandeb corridor.

articles.title: Red Sea Crisis: Wikipedia Update on Recent Military Operations
articles.description: Summary of Red Sea escalation timeline through April 2026, including March 15 US strikes in Yemen killing 53 and April 17 Ras Issa strike killing 74.

articles.title: Houthis Claim Ben Gurion Airport Strike
articles.description: Yahya Saree claims responsibility for cluster missile and drone attack on Israel's Ben Gurion airport in Jaffa.

articles.title: Houthis Resume Missile Attacks on Israel
articles.description: Brigadier-General Yahya Saree announced barrage of ballistic missiles targeting Israeli military sites; warned operations continue until aggression against resistance fronts ceases.

articles.title: Houthis Signal Blockade of Bab al-Mandeb as Strategic Option
articles.description: Deputy Information Minister Mohammed Mansour stated closing Bab al-Mandeb strait is among Houthi options; 30% of Israeli imports pass through waterway.

articles.title: Red Sea Crisis: Wikipedia Update on Recent Houthi Operations
articles.description: Timeline confirms Houthis resumed attacks on Israel March 28, 2026 amid 2026 Iran war; previously paused attacks from November 2025 after Gaza ceasefire.
